_notice: ALL RECORDS BELOW ARE FICTIONAL. This file illustrates the STRUCTURE of the Stripe-to-Dataverse reconciliation model (payout->Deposit, charge->Transaction, donor matching, exceptions, duplicate prevention, and reconciliation). It contains no production data, no real donor, no real Stripe object, and no real amount. Every id, name, and value was invented for demonstration.
_evidence_tier: Invented sample. Field concepts reconstruct the documented model; values are fictional. No production record value is represented.
_confidence_note: Confidence labels below (high/medium/low) are INVENTED demonstrations for readability. They are NOT the historical four-tier thresholds, which are not preserved and are not reconstructed here.

normalized_email_example.note: Fictional. Shows trim + lowercase normalization concept only. The only email domain used in this sample is the RFC-reserved example.invalid; no real address appears.

deposits.note: Fictional Deposit created from a fictional Stripe payout. Reference is a placeholder, not a real Stripe payout id.

scenarios.note: Fictional. Illustrates the strongest matching signal. Confidence label is invented, not a historical threshold.

scenarios.outcome: candidate proposed, routed for human review
scenarios.note: Fictional. A weaker signal proposes a candidate but does not auto-commit.
Fictional. No signal resolved to an existing Contact.

scenarios.match_signal: normalized email matched more than one Contact
scenarios.note: Fictional. Ambiguous matches leave the automatic path by design.
Fictional. Idempotency check by processor identifier prevents duplicates on rerun.

scenarios.outcome: Stripe payout represented as a Dataverse Deposit
scenarios.note: Fictional. A payout becomes the bank-level Deposit record.

scenarios.outcome: Transaction bound to its Deposit via the Dataverse identifier
scenarios.note: Fictional. Links a charge-derived Transaction to its payout-derived Deposit.

scenarios.outcome: processor settlement, bank deposit, and CRM transaction agree
scenarios.note: Fictional. Demonstrates an explicitly reconciled Transaction/Deposit relationship.

exception_examples.note: Fictional. A charge whose payout has not settled cannot bind to a Deposit yet.
